body:
Commenced a third share buyback programme worth up to GBP10.0m.

Buyback will end on or before 31 December; purpose stated as reducing share capital with intent to cancel all repurchased shares.

Appointed Investec Bank PLC to conduct the programme.

Company can repurchase up to 16.5m shares, under authority granted at its AGM.

Current stock price referenced: 222.10p, +6.0% on Wednesday; 12-month +25%.

Extended the deadline for Alamadiyaf al-Masiyyah for Trading LLC to announce a firm offer or walk away (per UK takeover rules).

Previous deadline would have expired at 5pm London time “today”; new deadline set to 6 May.

Extension rationale: gives the bidder (member of the Cafani Group) additional time to progress funding arrangements “as requested”.

Capricorn says it has received multiple unsolicited takeover proposals.

Capricorn had provided due diligence access earlier this year after evaluating multiple proposals.

Share price reaction referenced: +3.2% to 325.00p on Wednesday afternoon.

Independent non-executive director Martin Cooke to step down 30 April.

Deputy Chair Graham Oldroyd will revert to his former role as independent non-executive director.

Chair Stephen Harris quote: “Given that we now have a stable platform for growth the board will be focusing on business opportunities and returning to a more common governance structure...We are actively pursuing the recruitment of a permanent chief executive officer and will update shareholders on progress with this search as soon as we have something able to.”

Chair Jim Millen urges shareholders to vote against the resolution to change the board composition at the requisitioned GM (29 April).

Chair Jim Millen quote: “The board rejects the suggestion that the company has failed to create value. In addition to delivering specialist services to clients, the company has built valuable intellectual property (Virtual Tumour and our personalised dosing tool which is in development), proprietary know-how, modelling capability, scientific credibility and customer relationships that represent important strategic assets of the business,”

Millen adds there is “a genuine risk” that disruption to current stewardship without continuity of relevant company/sector knowledge could impair the ability to commercialise the value created.

time_period: Value creation plan with a four-year time horizon
body:
• Announces a value creation plan to "align executive leadership rewards directly with the delivery of sustained shareholder value".
  • Updates the executive board's long-term incentive arrangement via a one-off award "designed to incentivise the pursuit of strategic growth opportunities".
  • Plan design links (and increases) executive compensation to share price milestone achievement.
  • Executive board "value creation pool" rises to GBP12.3 million if the share price reaches GBP2.75.
  • Executive board "value creation pool" rises to GBP20.5 million if the share price reaches GBP3.0.
  • The GBP20.5 million pool is GBP8.2 million higher than the GBP12.3 million pool (+66.7%).
  • CEO Stephnie Judge receives 25% of the overall incentive pool regardless of share price (implies GBP3.075 million at the GBP12.3m pool; GBP5.125 million at the GBP20.5m pool).
  • CFO Daniel Barton receives 20% of the overall incentive pool regardless of share price (implies GBP2.46 million at the GBP12.3m pool; GBP4.10 million at the GBP20.5m pool).
  • Implied remaining allocation to other executive board members is 55% of the overall pool.

time_period: Order delivery through May 2028; revenue recognition from H2 2026
body:
• Awarded $63.5 million of orders for its titanium stress joint (TSJ) product line for a new offshore development in Guyana.
  • Award to be completed by the Group's Subsea Spring business unit.
  • Deliveries scheduled through to May 2028.
  • Revenue expected to be recognised from H2 2026.
  • New TSJ order is in addition to $4.4 million of orders already secured for the same development since December 2025 (via Stafford and Flexible Engineered Solutions).
  • Total orders disclosed for this development since December 2025 are $67.9 million ($63.5m + $4.4m).
  • The $63.5m TSJ order is $59.1m higher than the $4.4m previously disclosed (+1,343%, or ~14.4x).
  • Company signals "further incremental orders likely to be received throughout the rest of 2026."
  • Positions TSJ adoption on FPSO projects as supporting a strategy to capture more subsea spend by leveraging an enlarged product offering across the well life cycle.
  • CEO Jim Johnson said: "Our TSJ product line, which is a critical component for offshore developments, continues to be adopted on key offshore projects utilising FPSOs, given the maintenance benefits and reliability offered. We would like to thank our partner in Guyana for its continued confidence in our product offering and look forward to working together in the future with our range of products and solutions. This order also contributes to our guided subsea product group revenue and EBITDA through to 2028, as published at our subsea investor event held in January 2026."

time_period: Modified Dutch Auction tender offer expired April 1, 2026
body:
• Announced final results of its "modified Dutch Auction" tender offer, which expired at one minute after 11:59 P.M., New York City time, on April 1, 2026.
  • Deposit count (Equiniti Trust Company, LLC): 17,577,250 ordinary shares were properly tendered and not properly withdrawn at or below $92.00 per share.
  • Notes that not all shares tendered via notice of guaranteed delivery were delivered within the required settlement period.
  • Accepted for payment 17,577,250 shares (all properly tendered and not properly withdrawn) at $92.00 per share.
  • Aggregate repurchase cost stated as ~$1.617 billion, excluding fees and expenses.
  • Repurchased shares represent ~29.7% of shares outstanding as of April 1, 2026.
  • Implied total shares outstanding as of April 1, 2026 are ~59.2 million (based on 17.577m being 29.7%).
  • Nir Zohar, President and Co-founder, stated: "I am very pleased with the outcome of our Dutch auction tender offer, which is enabling us to repurchase nearly 30% of our market capitalization and return meaningful value to investors."

Publish Date Not provided in excerpt

Time Period Value creation plan with a four-year time horizon

• Announces a value creation plan to “align executive leadership rewards directly with the delivery of sustained shareholder value”.

• Updates the executive board’s long-term incentive arrangement via a one-off award “designed to incentivise the pursuit of strategic growth opportunities”.

• Plan design links (and increases) executive compensation to share price milestone achievement.

• Executive board “value creation pool” rises to GBP12.3 million if the share price reaches GBP2.75.

• Executive board “value creation pool” rises to GBP20.5 million if the share price reaches GBP3.0.

• The GBP20.5 million pool is GBP8.2 million higher than the GBP12.3 million pool (+66.7%).

• CEO Stephnie Judge receives 25% of the overall incentive pool regardless of share price (implies GBP3.075 million at the GBP12.3m pool; GBP5.125 million at the GBP20.5m pool).

• CFO Daniel Barton receives 20% of the overall incentive pool regardless of share price (implies GBP2.46 million at the GBP12.3m pool; GBP4.10 million at the GBP20.5m pool).

• Implied remaining allocation to other executive board members is 55% of the overall pool (based on the stated CEO/CFO fixed percentages).

Publish Date Not provided in excerpt (“today announces”)

Time Period Order delivery through May 2028; revenue recognition from H2 2026

• Awarded $63.5 million of orders for its titanium stress joint (TSJ) product line for a new offshore development in Guyana.

• Award to be completed by the Group’s Subsea Spring business unit.

• Deliveries scheduled through to May 2028.

• Revenue expected to be recognised from H2 2026.

• New TSJ order is in addition to $4.4 million of orders already secured for the same development since December 2025 (via Stafford and Flexible Engineered Solutions).

• Total orders disclosed for this development since December 2025 are $67.9 million ($63.5m + $4.4m).

• The $63.5m TSJ order is $59.1m higher than the $4.4m previously disclosed (+1,343%, or ~14.4x).

• Company signals “further incremental orders likely to be received throughout the rest of 2026.”

• Positions TSJ adoption on FPSO projects as supporting a strategy to capture more subsea spend by leveraging an enlarged product offering across the well life cycle.

• CEO Jim Johnson said:“Our TSJ product line, which is a critical component for offshore developments, continues to be adopted on key offshore projects utilising FPSOs, given the maintenance benefits and reliability offered.

“We would like to thank our partner in Guyana for its continued confidence in our product offering and look forward to working together in the future with our range of products and solutions.

“This order also contributes to our guided subsea product group revenue and EBITDA through to 2028, as published at our subsea investor event held in January 2026.”

Publish Date N/A (not stated in provided text)

Time Period N/A (workplace award announcement)

• Named a 2026 USA Today Top Workplaces award winner for the sixth consecutive year.

• Ranked #4 in the 1,000–2,499 employee size category (Top 10 again this year).

• Also recognised in the Top Workplaces “Well-Being” specialty category.

• Quote (CEO Vinayak Hegde): “Being a Top Workplace reflects the commitment of our team members… and deliver on our mission of changing lives for dealers, consumers, and each other.”

• Quote (CEO Vinayak Hegde) on culture/remote work: “In a remote work environment like ours, it’s especially important that we remain intentional about connecting and collaborating across teams and living our PRIDE values…”

• Operational detail: nearly 95% of team is fully remote.

• Connectivity initiatives cited: annual Sales Leadership Exchange, Support and Operations Alignment Retreat, plus in-office days and regional roundtable meetings.

• “Second workplace award… this year”: earlier in the month, reached #18 on the Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For® list (highest-ever ranking for the company).

• Other accolades listed over the past year include (non-exhaustive): Fortune Best Workplace for Financial Services and Insurance™, Best Workplace for Women™, People Magazine’s Companies that Care®, Top Workplace for Remote Work, and Best Place to Work in IT (Computerworld).

• Methodology: Top Workplaces awards based on an Energage employee survey measuring “Workplace Experience Themes” linked to high performance.

Publish Date Not stated (Alliance News report filed “Thursday”)

Time Period Proposed refinery disposal (completion targeted H2 2026) + regulatory review

Germany’s Economy Ministry is reviewing BP’s planned sale of its Gelsenkirchen refinery to Klesch Group (per Der Spiegel).

Ministry spokesperson declined to comment, citing trade/business secrets.

Germany may prohibit acquisitions by non-European investors under certain conditions (context for the review).

BP previously announced it had struck an agreement for Klesch Group to acquire the refinery; completion targeted second half of 2026, subject to regulatory approval.

Refinery detail: site annual processing capacity ~12 million tons of crude oil; primarily produces road and aviation transport fuels.

Der Spiegel allegation: Klesch Group controlled via a network of subsidiaries registered in Jersey (described as a tax haven).

Market reaction noted: BP shares +2.1% to 574.70p on the day mentioned, alongside higher Brent oil prices.

Publish Date Not stated (Alliance News report filed “Thursday”)

Time Period Announced acquisition agreement (closing expected later in 2026)

Announced an agreement to acquire Gruns Nutrition Inc (gummy-form supplements), as part of portfolio shift “towards premium and high growth spaces”.

Product scope: Gruns provides nutrient-support gummies for adults and children.

Unilever’s positioning of target + origin story: “Founded in 2023 by Chad Janis, Gruns has a clear mission to make daily nutrient support an easy, enjoyable ritual. Janis launched the company after recognising that the supplement industry's biggest barrier wasn't awareness, but adherence. In a short time, Gruns has become one of the largest brands in the US Greens Supplement category, building a digitally native, culture-driven brand that resonates with consumers,” Unilever said.

Strategic framing quote: “This marks another step in optimising Unilever's portfolio towards premium and high growth spaces, with a stronger focus on Beauty & Wellbeing and the US market.”

Deal terms: not disclosed.

Expected closing: later this year (2026), subject to regulatory approvals.

Market reaction noted: Unilever shares -1.0% to 4,254.50p on the day mentioned.

Note: this item appears twice in the provided pack (Articles 26 and 27) with the same content.

company: Nightfood Holdings Inc. (OTC: NGTF) / TechForce Robotics (industry editorial coverage)
body:
Publish Date Not stated in excerpt

Time Period Broad industry commentary; references current regulatory shift + recent company initiatives

Editorial frames EU GMP Annex 1 as updated guidance emphasising minimising human involvement in sterile production and encouraging barrier systems + automation to reduce contamination risk.

Editorial claims FDA inspections continue to identify recurring compliance gaps (notably aseptic processing/documentation) and positions automation/data integrity as a growing compliance driver.

Company-specific angle: TechForce Robotics described as building AI-enabled robotic platforms with SOP-based intelligence and real-time deviation detection aimed at regulated environments.

Partnership noted: Nightfood/TechForce Robotics is said to be partnering with Oncotelic Therapeutics to advance commercialisation of a PDAOAI-enabled, GMP-compliant robotics platform.

Quote included in the piece: “This milestone reflects the culmination of several years of research and development efforts, resulting in an integrated platform designed to combine Oncotelic's proprietary PDAOAI capabilities with TechForce's robotics hardware and manufacturing expertise. The system under development is designed to operate within GMP-regulated environments and is intended to enable automated material handling, real-time monitoring and PDAOAI-enhanced compliance workflows across pharmaceutical manufacturing and related applications.”

Larger peer-name “context” included by the editorial (presented as examples of the trend, not company filings): mentions claimed initiatives involving NVIDIA, Johnson & Johnson, Amazon/AWS, and Tesla in physical AI/robotics-enabled workflows.

Publish Date Not stated in provided extract (AINewsWire editorial)

Time Period Industry / regulatory backdrop + company positioning update (no explicit period stated)

• Editorial frames pharma manufacturing as undergoing “structural transformation” driven by tighter contamination control, data integrity and traceability expectations (EU GMP Annex 1; FDA inspection findings).

• Core positioning claim: TechForce Robotics is developing AI-enabled robotic platforms combining autonomous functionality with SOP-based intelligence and real-time deviation detection, aimed at “continuous” compliance in GMP-regulated environments.

• The piece argues existing automation “has not fully addressed” persistent compliance gaps (aseptic processing, documentation), implying a need for more context-aware / adaptive robotics vs “predefined task” robots.

• States Nightfood Holdings (via TechForce Robotics) is partnering with Oncotelic Therapeutics on commercialising a “PDAOAI-enabled, GMP-compliant robotics platform.”

• Full quote (collaboration milestone): “This milestone reflects the culmination of several years of research and development efforts, resulting in an integrated platform designed to combine Oncotelic's proprietary PDAOAI capabilities with TechForce's robotics hardware and manufacturing expertise,” the announcement stated. “The system under development is designed to operate within GMP-regulated environments and is intended to enable automated material handling, real-time monitoring and PDAOAI-enhanced compliance workflows across pharmaceutical manufacturing and related applications.”

• Feed note: the same AINewsWire editorial appears multiple times in this file under different tickers (e.g., AMZN / NVDA contexts), but the profiled issuer content is essentially the same.

Publish Date Not stated explicitly in provided extract (Business Wire style release; references “recently approved”)

Time Period Product availability launch + delivery expansion (current; expansion “by year end”)

• Amazon Pharmacy now offers Eli Lilly’s GLP-1 pill Foundayo™ (once-daily oral for adults with obesity or overweight with weight-related medical problems).

• Pricing disclosed: with insurance “as low as $1 per day / $25 per month”; cash pay “$5 per day / $149 per month,” with manufacturer-sponsored coupons applied automatically pre-checkout.

• Delivery footprint: Same-Day Delivery in “nearly 3,000 cities and towns,” expanding to “nearly 4,500 by year end” (implies +~1,500 locations / ~50% increase).

• Service proposition vs mail-order: in remote areas, Amazon cites next-/two-/three-day options versus “five to 10 days” for other mail-order delivery services.

• New channel: plans “soon” for in-office, licensed pharmacist-supported kiosks at select One Medical sites, enabling pickup “within minutes” after appointments.

• Amazon Pharmacy quote (Tanvi Patel): “We are giving customers faster treatment options with our Same-Day Delivery offering, easy ways to save with automatically applied manufacturer coupons and the price transparency they need to make informed decisions about their care.”

• Lilly quote (Laura Steele): “If prescribed, patients may be able to have Foundayo on their doorstep the same day or walk out of their doctor's office with it in-hand in Los Angeles — eliminating the waiting and uncertainty that patients may face when starting their treatment journey.”

• Additional metrics claimed: Amazon Pharmacy says customers have saved “more than $200 million” through automatic coupons, with GLP-1 medications the largest savings category; it also notes GLP-1 delivery since 2021 and an existing collaboration with LillyDirect for insured customers.

• Non-NII: Citi forecasts +9% growth in non-net interest income in 2026.

• Citi says the principal investor pushback is macro/political (UK outlook) rather than company-specific.

• Political comments included: poor Labour local election showing “widely anticipated”; leadership change “remains unlikely”; UK fiscal position “better than feared” (better positioned for an energy shock).

• Citi also notes: loan demand remains strong (esp. large corporates); unemployment rising but mainly among under-24s (small share of bank lending).

• Market data included in note: current stock price 99.93p; 12-month change +55%.

• Article appears twice in the pack (Articles 37 and 38) with the same content.

BP p.l.c.

Publish Date Not stated in the excerpt (Alliance News company news)

Time Period New offshore Egypt gas discovery (reported “on Wednesday” with market move “on Thursday morning”)

• BP reported a new gas deposit offshore Egypt (shared interest with Eni).

• Discovery well: Denise W-1, part of the Temsah concession (Eastern Mediterranean).

• Eni preliminary estimates: ~2 trillion cubic feet gas initially in place + 130 million barrels associated condensates.

• Location: ~70km offshore, 95m water depth; <10km from existing infrastructure.

• Reservoir quality/thickness: “High-quality gas-bearing sandstone reservoirs” across a ~90m gross interval.

• BP positioning: says it’s BP’s second exploration discovery this year, following 12 discoveries in 2025.

• Temsah concession: BP previously entered a binding agreement (July) with Egyptian state entities for a 20-year renewal.

• Working interest: BP 50% and Eni 50%; Eni operates the Denise development lease via Petrobel (Eni/EGPC JV).

• Other Egypt positions mentioned: BP took 100% of North-East El Alamein offshore concession and 25% of West El Hammad; also signed for Red Sea Block 6.

• Market/context datapoints included: Brent referenced at $97.40/bbl vs $95.20 prior close; BP shares +2.3% to 575.78p (also +13% past month; +73% past year).

• Corporate note included: appointment of new BP CEO Meg O’Neill and Deputy CEO Carol Howle referenced as occurring “last week”.

Publish Date Not stated in the excerpt (Alliance News company news)

Time Period Partnership announcement; rollout target stated for financial 2026

• New partnership with Asda Stores Ltd to install/operate laundry machines across Asda Supercentre, Superstore, supermarket and petrol forecourt sites in the UK.

• Scale target: up to 700 laundry machines across these Asda locations (described as high-footfall).

• Strategic linkage: ME says this supports its growth plan to install 1,300 laundry machines in financial 2026.

• Long-term ambition stated: install >20,000 laundry machines globally.

• Current footprint stated: >7,600 machines across 12 countries; >1,500 in the UK & Republic of Ireland.

• Deputy CEO Vladimir Crasneanscki quote: “Asda is a perfect company to partner with, their retail and petrol stations are excellent locations for our services and their innovative and fast-paced approach will enable us to deploy our units rapidly.”

• Deputy CEO Vladimir Crasneanscki quote: “We are delighted to announce this partnership; this is the largest single client deal in the history of our laundry division and demonstrates the growing demand for our services.”

• Share price reaction in the piece: +3.7% to 147.00p (Thursday morning).

Publish Date Not stated in the excerpt (Alliance News company news)

Time Period Completion of a leasing campaign (at SIX St Andrew Street, Farringdon)

• Let the final remaining office space at SIX St Andrew Street (Farringdon, London) — 3rd and 4th floors, totaling >11,680 sq ft — to an AI company.

• Building now fully occupied and generating >£8.8m annual rent at an average of £200/sq ft.

• Letting metrics: rent level stated as 6.2% ahead of ERV and 6.2% yield on cost.

• Portfolio mix datapoint: AI-related customers represent 11.7% of total office portfolio, or 25.2% of fully managed offices.

• GPE quote: “This letting reflects the strong momentum in GPE's fully managed portfolio, where leasing activity accelerated during the final quarter of the financial year… with 19 deals completed and a further seven currently under offer.”

• Molly Maguire (Senior Flex Leasing manager) quote: “Completing the leasing campaign at SIX marks a great milestone for our Fully Managed portfolio. Demand continues to be driven by customers looking for high quality, flexible workspace in well-connected central locations, and SIX has proven to be an excellent example of how that proposition resonates.”

• Share price move in the piece: -0.9% to 305.80p (Thursday morning).

Publish Date N/A (not stated in provided text)

Time Period N/A (community launch announcement)

• Announced grand opening of Topaz at Skye Canyon, a new townhome community within the Skye Canyon development in Las Vegas.

• Community size: 49 homesites.

• Pricing: homes start from the “$380s.”

• Product range: 3- and 4-bedroom townhome floor plans with attached two-car garages.

• Four floor plans disclosed with sizes: 1,256 / 1,432 / 1,533 / 1,665 sq ft.

• Includes “CompleteHome Plus™” upgrade package (e.g., energy-efficient Whirlpool stainless steel appliances, 42” wood cabinetry, tile backsplash, designer finishes).

• Quote (Ron Christian, VP Operations – Nevada): “Expanding into Skye Canyon is an exciting opportunity for LGI Homes… It’s the perfect combination of new construction and a proven community setting where homeowners can feel at home from the very first day.”

Publish Date Not stated in provided excerpt (included in an Alliance News “trading updates round-up”).

Time Period Current product launch (no financial period specified).

Launched integrated royalty-free music from Epidemic Sound inside (Blackbird’s browser-based collaborative video editor).

All users can now access Epidemic Sound’s catalogue directly within the editor (search/preview/add tracks without leaving the workflow).

Licensing is handled at export, and can be tailored to user requirements.

Chair Ian McDonough quote: “Music is fundamental to great storytelling, it's what gives video content its emotion and energy. So rather than sending creators off to find tracks elsewhere, we've built it straight into the platform. World-class music, right there in users' workflows. No interruptions, just creativity and complete peace of mind. This is a huge moment for the company and we're incredibly excited.”

Publish Date Not stated in provided excerpt (included in an Alliance News “trading updates round-up”).

Time Period Drilling early April 2026; assay results expected progressively through Q2 2026.

JV partner West Coast Silver Ltd plans to start a 1,500-metre diamond drilling programme in early April at Elizabeth Hill Silver Project (near Karratha, Pilbara, Western Australia).

Drill programme expected duration: 4–6 weeks.

Assay results expected progressively throughout Q2.

Drilling focus: test for extensions of mineralisation along strike of the historic Elizabeth Hill high-grade underground silver mine, plus structural targets along the Munni Munni Fault.

Chair Bruce Garlick quote: “Our previous 2025 drilling confirmed that silver mineralisation at Elizabeth Hill extends beyond the historical underground mine and associated with a broader structural corridor along the Munni Munni Fault. This current program of diamond drilling below Elizabeth Hill is designed to determine whether there are high-grade silver extensions below and south of the mine. The drilling will also test whether high-grade mineralisation repeats at depth; a model that, if confirmed, could materially expand the resource potential.”

company: GreenTech Metals Ltd (JV partner update relevant to Alien Metals’ Munni Munni exposure)
body:
Publish Date Not stated in provided excerpt (included in an Alliance News “trading updates round-up”).

Time Period Current exploration update (no financial period specified).

GreenTech reports “material upside potential” at Munni Munni Platinum-Palladium-Copper-Nickel Project not captured in the historic mineral resource estimate.

Historic MRE cited: 24 million tonnes @ 2.9 g/t (Pt, Pd, Rh & Au) for 2.2 million ounces total.

Upside items highlighted: high-grade PGE zones above 4 g/t.

Upside items highlighted: under-recognised copper and nickel mineralisation outside reef domains.

Upside items highlighted: near-surface eastern mineralisation that may be suitable for open-pit mining.

Publish Date Not stated in provided excerpt (included in an Alliance News “trading updates round-up”).

Time Period Goals for the next 12 months.

Targets launch of multiple processing hubs across Tanzania, Africa, and Latin America.

Plans to scale mineral aggregation network via new artisanal & small-scale miners and semi-industrial partnerships.

Intends to secure long-term agreements aligned with EU and US critical mineral requirements.

Plans to progress ventures on high-scale assets confirmed through rapid validation protocols.

Company comment quote: “The synergy between Amigo's visionary leadership and aggressive operational execution has created a platform of Tier-One potential. By controlling the processing nodes and the information layer of the African mineral economy, we are delivering a new standard of transparency and growth. We stand at a pivotal inflection point, uniquely positioned to capture the multi-billion-dollar industrial upside of African mineral beneficiation.”

Publish Date Not stated in provided excerpt (included in an Alliance News “trading updates round-up”).

Time Period Buyback restart (no financial period specified).

Restarted share buyback programme.

Notes there is no guarantee the programme will be implemented in full.

Publish Date Not stated in provided excerpt (included in an Alliance News “trading updates round-up”).

Confirms all conditions relating to its conditional placing are now satisfied.

February placing raised GBP7.6 million gross at 4.0p per share.

Conditionality referenced: in part dependent on EIS/VCT legislative changes (announced in the UK Government Autumn Statement) passing into law.

Publish Date Not stated in provided excerpt (included in an Alliance News “trading updates round-up”).

Time Period Extended production test to run “over the coming weeks”.

Began an extended production test at Sagebrush-1 well (Colorado).

Testing expected to run over the coming weeks; results to be announced after analysis is completed.

CEO Howard McLaughlin quote: “Commencing the extended production test at Sagebrush-1 is a major milestone for Quantum and an important step forward for the company. This well has already demonstrated the presence of helium, and this programme is focused on confirming flow rates and commercial potential. The results will be critical in moving our prospective resources towards reserves and defining the pathway to development.”

Time Period Option exercise notice (no financial period specified).

Received notice of the exercise of options over 300,000 shares, raising GBP42,000.

Exercise price stated as 0.14p per share; note this appears arithmetically inconsistent with GBP42,000 proceeds (GBP42,000 over 300,000 shares implies 14p per share).

Received requisite consents in a consent solicitation related to USD1.29bn 10.25% senior secured notes due 2026.

Brent oil referenced at USD95.20/bbl (Wednesday afternoon) vs USD110.24/bbl around the same time the prior day (-13.6% day-on-day).

Current stock price referenced: 12.46p, -3.7% on Wednesday; 12-month -8.9%.

Shareholders approved proposed capital reduction plan.

Notes that a resolution to grant directors authority to allot shares was withdrawn.

Share prices referenced: Johannesburg 74c (+4.2%) with 12-month -1.3%; London 3.23p (+1.1%) with 12-month +14%.

Publish Date Not stated in provided extract (Alliance News “IN BRIEF”)

Time Period Director/PCA dealing (announcement day)

• Rebecca Papp (person closely associated with Group Merchandise & Operations Director Richard Papp) sold 4,033 shares at £129.25 each.

• Total value of sale: £521,265.

• “Current share price” cited: 13,305p (≈ £133.05), implying the cited market price is ~+2.9% above the disclosed sale price.

• “12-month change” cited: +21%.

Publish Date Not stated in provided extract (Alliance News “EXTRA”)

Time Period Q1 2026 trading update (guidance ranges)

• Integrated Gas output expected 880k–920k boepd in Q1, down from 948k boepd in Q4 2025 (midpoint implies ~-5.1% QoQ).

• Integrated Gas guidance reduced vs prior range 920k–980k boepd (new lower end -40k / ~-4.3% vs old lower end; new upper end -60k / ~-6.1% vs old upper end).

• Shell attributes Integrated Gas weakness to “the impact of the Middle East conflict on Qatari volumes” and flags: “in light of the ongoing situation in the Middle East, the outlook provided is subject to increased uncertainty.”

• LNG volume guidance nudged up to 7.6–8.0mt from 7.4–8.0mt (lower end +0.2mt / ~+2.7%); Q4 LNG volumes were 7.8mt.

• LNG drivers: “ramp-up of LNG Canada” offset by “Australia weather constraints” and “Qatar LNG outages.”

• Upstream production expected 1.76–1.86m boepd, down from 1.89m boepd in Q4 (midpoint implies ~-4.2% QoQ); includes reduced production after the Adura JV incorporation.

• Chemicals & Products indicative refining margin: $17/bbl vs $14/bbl in Q4 (+21.4% QoQ).

• Trading statement language: “trading & optimisation is expected to be significantly higher” than Q4.

• Balance sheet: non-cash net debt expected to be impacted by a $3bn–$4bn increase in “variable components of long-term shipping leases.”

• Working capital expected to “swing” to negative $10m–$15m in Q1 vs positive $1.3bn in the prior quarter (as stated in the article).

• Market/analyst colour included in the piece: AJ Bell describes a “dichotomy” where higher energy prices help profits but Middle East footprint disrupts operations; JPMorgan flags a pricing vs balance sheet “timing mis-match” and a “heavy… working capital build.”

• Share price / macro context cited: Shell shares -5.3% to 3,377.70p; Brent crude cited at $93.76/bbl vs $110.24 at prior London close (about -15.0%) after the US and Iran agreed a two-week ceasefire.

Publish Date Not stated in provided extract (Alliance News “EXTRA” + separate shorter item)

Time Period FCA motor finance redress scheme update (current)

• Roll-out ambition: target up to 700 machines across ASDA sites (described as the “overall ambition”).

• Current footprint: 7,600+ machines across 12 countries; 1,500+ located in the UK & Republic of Ireland.

• Product spec highlights: self-service, large-capacity 9kg and 20kg drums; energy-efficient, high-speed wash/dry; available 24/7.

• FY 2026 ambition: install more than 1,300 machines.

• Long-term ambition: 20,000+ machines globally.

• Stated 2025 delivery: “record” installations of 1,326 across geographies.

• Quote (Vladimir Crasneanscki, Deputy CEO): “ASDA is a perfect company to partner with… this is the largest single client deal in the history of our laundry division and demonstrates the growing demand for our services.”

• Quote (Vladimir Crasneanscki, Deputy CEO): “The rapid expansion of our laundry services is a key strategic priority… This partnership is indicative of our intention to further cement our UK market-leading position in the unattended laundry segment.”

time_period: FCA motor finance redress scheme update (April 2026) + restructuring and HY results context

  • Shore Capital: downgraded to hold, "Take profits" — "underlying operational performance remains weak".

time_period: FCA motor finance redress scheme update (April 2026) — shorter item
body:

Time Period FCA motor finance redress scheme impact review (pro forma as at 31 January 2026; rollout summer 2026–end 2027)

• Reviewed FCA Policy Statement PS26/3 (motor finance consumer redress scheme) and its impact; stated the published scheme’s estimated cost is c.£320m, “broadly similar” to existing provision and “can be comfortably absorbed” by existing capital resources.

• No provision changes recognised yet; existing IAS 37 provision remains under review.

• Estimated scheme cost (if recognised in isolation) implies pro forma provision c.£320m as at 31 Jan 2026 vs IAS 37 provision £294m (difference +£26m / +8.8%).

• Capital impact: would reduce CET1 ratio by c.25 bps to 14.0% (pro forma at 31 Jan 2026), still above medium-term target 12–13%.

• Provision methodology: current £294m based on probability-weighted scenarios (including an implementation scenario aligned to the FCA’s 7 Oct 2025 consultation); the £320m estimate reflects the policy statement scenario only and changes relative to the consultation.

• Key assumption: c.720k qualifying UK regulated motor finance loans written 6 Apr 2007–1 Nov 2024, including c.640k DCA loans + c.80k non-DCA loans likely meeting “tied” and/or “high commission” criteria.

• Key assumption: average redress payment c.£500 incl. compensatory interest (vs industry average £829 cited by FCA), reflecting smaller loan sizes and lower commission levels in Close Brothers’ book.

• Key assumption: 75% claim rate; each ±5% absolute change in claim rate drives ±c.£18m change in estimate (incl. delivery cost impact).

• Key assumption: delivery cost c.£66m, excluding c.£14m of costs already incurred against the existing provision.

• Key assumption: scheme rollout from summer 2026 to end-CY 2027, implying a time-value discount of c.£16m.

• Footnote mechanics: the £294m balance sheet provision at 31 Jan 2026 reflects £300m charged to income statement, plus £8m partial unwind of discount (time value), less £14m costs incurred to date.

Time Period FY ended 31 March 2026 (FY26 trading update ahead of June 2026 results)

• FY26 retail volumes c.65,000, +8.0% YoY (record volumes).

• Profit before tax expected c.£7.5m, +83% YoY (FY25: £4.1m); attributed to record metal margin performance offsetting inflationary pressures.

• EBITDA expected c.£27.0m, +13.0% YoY (FY25: £23.9m).

• ROCE expected c.70%, positioned as evidence the “capital light model” is working.

• Net Promoter Score improved to 83 (FY25: 80, +3 pts / +3.8%).

• Outperformance vs used-car market: Motorpoint volumes grew 6.0% in calendar 2025 vs 2.2% for overall used-car market.

• AI tooling: since launching an “agentic AI tool” to reactivate historic closed quotes, c.900 incremental vehicle sales attributed to that channel.

• Supply/pricing: vehicle supply “more normalised”, enabling more bulk deals; increased sourcing via Sell Your Car channel; data-led buying/pricing described as embedded and supporting record metal margins (helping offset labour cost inflation).

• New store: intends to open a Leeds store in Summer 2026.

• CEO Mark Carpenter quote: “The Group had an excellent year in FY26, and I am delighted to report record sales volumes and an 83% increase in profit before taxation. FY26 has been a step change year for Motorpoint, where the use of data became fundamental within the business and we started to embrace the benefits of AI. Whilst the macroeconomic uncertainty in recent weeks leads to a degree of caution due to the risks of increased inflation and interest rates, our superior customer service, omnichannel business model and exciting growth plans mean we are well placed to take advantage of opportunities to further increase market share and build long term value for shareholders.”

• Appointed Michael Rogers as Chief Financial Officer and Treasurer, effective 1 June 2026.

• Rogers’ current roles: CFO of Conlan Tire Co., Hercules Materials Holdings LLC, and certain privately held affiliates.

• Rogers’ prior experience: ~30 years at Ford Motor Company in finance leadership roles (incl. Model e Controller – Product Development; Global Controller – Warranty; Global Controller – Material Planning & Logistics; Finance Director for Canada/Mexico/South America Ops).

• Outgoing CFO: Jude M. Beres will resign as CFO & Treasurer effective 29 May 2026 (pursuing opportunities outside transportation/logistics) and is expected to stay through that date to support transition.

• Quote (CEO Tim Phillips) on incoming CFO: “Mike brings tremendous breadth and depth of financial, operational and leadership experience… We believe Mike's background, judgment and familiarity with Universal will make him an outstanding addition to our senior leadership team."

• Quote (CEO Tim Phillips) on outgoing CFO: “Jude has served as our Chief Financial Officer and Treasurer since 2016… Over the course of nearly three decades with the Company and its affiliated businesses, Jude has played an important role in our growth and development…”

• “Requisite Consents” threshold was 90% and was achieved on 7 April 2026.

• Consents cover: (i) indenture + intercreditor amendments, (ii) release of existing Notes and issuance of New Notes under an amended/restated indenture, and (iii) waivers to permit release of collateral in connection with the refinancing.

• Next steps: Tullow/trustee/others to execute a Second Supplemental Indenture (permit pro rata redemption, reduce minimum denominations, implement waiver) and a Third Supplemental Indenture (other proposed amendments); consents become non-revocable at the “Effective Time.”

• Operability: Second Supplemental Indenture becomes effective/operative at Effective Time; Third becomes effective then, but certain amendments don’t become operative until settlement conditions are met.

• Voting window remains open until 11:59pm (NYC) 21 April 2026 (unless extended/terminated/amended by Tullow).

• Economic terms for all holders (per announcement):– 23 April 2026: portion of existing Notes redeemed at par, pro rata, plus accrued/unpaid interest on the redeemed amount.– 24 April 2026: receive (a) New Notes, (b) accrued/unpaid interest on remaining existing Notes then outstanding, and (c) a 1.00% fee calculated on aggregate principal outstanding as of the Expiration Time.

• Non-cash net debt expected to be impacted by a $3–$4bn increase in “variable components of long-term shipping leases” in the current macro environment.

Time Period Q1 2026 (international volume statistics)

• International average daily volume (ADV) reached a record 11.4m contracts in Q1 2026, +30% YoY.

• Record international interest rate ADV of 5.7m contracts, +30% YoY.

• Other international asset-class quarterly records: metals +116%, energy +62%, agricultural +16%, equity index +11%, FX +1% (YoY).

• Regional highlights: EMEA ADV 8.4m (+29% YoY); APAC ADV 2.6m (+33% YoY); LatAm ADV 224k (+21% YoY); Canada ADV 219k (+12% YoY).

• Global ADV (all markets) reported at 36.2m contracts in Q1 2026, +22% YoY; stated this was also the first time quarterly volumes hit record levels across rates, energy, metals, equity index, agriculture and FX.

• Julie Winkler (Senior Managing Director & Chief Commercial Officer) quote: “This surge in trading activity demonstrates how our global client base is turning to CME Group to manage risk in real time, through our benchmark products and on a regulated marketplace… In this risk-always-on environment, our deeply liquid markets are critical for helping clients hedge their exposure and pursue opportunities across all asset classes and time zones.”

time_period: Order delivery through May 2028; revenue recognition from H2 2026
body:
• Won multiple orders for its titanium stress joint (TSJ) product line for a new offshore development in Guyana.
  • Award value: USD63.5 million.
  • Delivery schedule: through May 2028.
  • Revenue recognition expected to start in H2 2026.
  • Work will be completed by the Subsea Spring business unit.
  • These awards are in addition to USD4.4 million of orders already secured for the same development by Hunting's Stafford and Flexible Engineered Solutions business units since end-2025.
  • Hunting expects further incremental orders for this Guyana development "throughout the remainder of 2026".
  • Hunting frames the awards as validating its TSJ solution on FPSO developments, citing "maintenance benefits and reliability".
  • CEO Jim Johnson said: "Our TSJ product line, which is a critical component for offshore developments, continues to be adopted on key offshore projects utilising FPSOs, given the maintenance benefits and reliability offered."
  • CEO Jim Johnson said: "This order also contributes to our guided subsea product group revenue and [earnings before interest, tax, depreciation, and amortisation] through to 2028, as published at our subsea investor event held in January 2026."

time_period: Deep level drilling programme (March–December 2025); resource/reserve update expected during 2026
body:
• Reported better-than-expected results from its deep level drilling programme at the Blanket mine (Zimbabwe).
  • Stated the orebodies returned grades and widths consistent with or exceeding expectations, prompting an intent to update the mineral resource estimate during 2026.
  • Said the results suggest gold mineralisation may extend deeper than previously anticipated.
  • Market reaction noted: shares up 5.7% to 1,850p (Tuesday morning, London).
  • Completed 10,312 metres of deep level drilling between March and December 2025.
  • Eroica orebody (≈1 km north of Blanket): results consistent with expectations.
  • Blanket orebody: identified a new intercept with "excellent" grades up to 9.2 g/t at 1,045m depth.
  • Newly discovered orebody has been named "Blanket 7".
  • Lima orebody: confirmed to "34 level", meaning gold present at 1,110m depth; Caledonia says this supports potential for further depth extensions.
  • Said deep drilling results will be incorporated into an updated mineral resource and mineral reserve statement to be released this year.
  • CEO said: "The latest results from our deep drilling programme reinforce the geological strength of Blanket mine and demonstrate the continuity of mineralisation at depth across multiple orebodies. The consistency of grades and widths we are seeing...provides growing confidence in the scale and quality of the mineral resource below the current lowest levels of the mine."
  • CEO said: "These outcomes support our longer term planning efforts and highlight the value of the investments we have made".

time_period: Share buyback programme from 07 April 2026 to on/before 31 December 2026
body:
• Announced commencement of a third share buyback programme (the "2026 Programme") following its FY results release on 17 March 2026.
  • Maximum aggregate market value to be bought back: £10,000,000 (excluding associated costs and stamp duty).
  • Programme start date: 07 April 2026.
  • Programme end date: on or before 31 December 2026.
  • Stated sole purpose: reduce share capital.
  • Company intends to cancel any shares purchased.
  • Entered into non-discretionary instructions with Investec Bank plc to conduct the programme.
  • Investec will act as principal and make trading decisions independently of Wickes.
  • Programme is within existing AGM authority, which at the date of announcement equals 16,502,960 ordinary shares.
  • Said the maximum aggregate number of shares purchasable will be updated to reflect authority granted at the 2026 AGM (19 May 2026).
  • Programme to be conducted in accordance with Market Abuse Regulation (596/2014) (as retained in UK law) and the FCA Listing Rules.

time_period: Director dealing disclosures covering purchases on 07 April 2026 and 01 April 2026
body:

time_period: Deep level drilling completed March–December 2025; targets resource confidence/extension beyond 34 level (1,110m depth)
body:
• Reports "better than expected grades and widths" from deep level drilling at Blanket Mine, supporting increased confidence in the overall mineral resource.
  • Deep drilling campaign completed 10,311.9 metres between March and December 2025.
  • Drilling objective: assess continuity of mineralised zones on the Blanket, Eroica and Lima orebodies to increase confidence in existing mineral resources.
  • Targeted growing the mineral resource estimate beyond 34 level, stated as 1,110 metres below surface.
  • States Blanket and Eroica orebodies returned grades/widths consistent with, or better than, expectations.
  • Notes multiple wide zones within newly identified Blanket 7 (BLK7) orebody as part of the "high grade intersections at depth" theme.
  • Says Lima orebody confirmed to 34 level, supporting potential depth extensions.
  • Expects results to increase mineral resource confidence and support future life-of-mine planning.
  • BLK7 (ARS1110EX2401): 13.02m true width at 9.24 g/t (intersection depth from surface 1045.3m).
  • BLK7 (ARS1110EX2402): 40.42m true width at 3.14 g/t (depth 1075.0m).
  • BLK7 (ARS1110EX2402, included interval): 6.38m true width at 9.90 g/t.
  • BLK7 (ARS1110EX2403): 46.28m true width at 4.25 g/t (depth 1082.0m).
  • BLK7 (ARS1110EX2403, included interval): 1.16m true width at 53.39 g/t.
  • BLK7 (ARS1110EX2404): 20.50m true width at 3.98 g/t (depth 1054.1m).
  • BQRHWN (ARS1110EX2503): 3.65m true width at 7.74 g/t (depth 1099.3m).
  • BQR (BLK1110EX2412): 7.17m true width at 7.15 g/t (depth 1083.0m).

Time Period Order delivery through May 2028; revenue recognition from H2 2026

• Won multiple orders for its titanium stress joint (TSJ) product line for a new offshore development in Guyana.

• Award value: USD63.5 million.

• Delivery schedule: through May 2028.

• Revenue recognition expected to start in H2 2026.

• Work will be completed by the Subsea Spring business unit.

• These awards are in addition to USD4.4 million of orders already secured for the same development by Hunting’s Stafford and Flexible Engineered Solutions business units since end-2025.

• Hunting expects further incremental orders for this Guyana development “throughout the remainder of 2026”.

• Hunting frames the awards as validating its TSJ solution on FPSO developments, citing “maintenance benefits and reliability”.

• CEO Jim Johnson said: “Our TSJ product line, which is a critical component for offshore developments, continues to be adopted on key offshore projects utilising FPSOs, given the maintenance benefits and reliability offered.”

• CEO Jim Johnson said: “This order also contributes to our guided subsea product group revenue and [earnings before interest, tax, depreciation, and amortisation] through to 2028, as published at our subsea investor event held in January 2026.”

Time Period Deep level drilling programme (March–December 2025); resource/reserve update expected during 2026

• Reported better-than-expected results from its deep level drilling programme at the Blanket mine (Zimbabwe).

• Stated the orebodies returned grades and widths consistent with or exceeding expectations, prompting an intent to update the mineral resource estimate during 2026.

• Said the results suggest gold mineralisation may extend deeper than previously anticipated.

• Market reaction noted: shares up 5.7% to 1,850p (Tuesday morning, London).

• Completed 10,312 metres of deep level drilling between March and December 2025.

• Eroica orebody (≈1 km north of Blanket): results consistent with expectations.

• Blanket orebody: identified a new intercept with “excellent” grades up to 9.2 g/t at 1,045m depth.

• Newly discovered orebody has been named “Blanket 7”.

• Lima orebody: confirmed to “34 level”, meaning gold present at 1,110m depth; Caledonia says this supports potential for further depth extensions.

• Said deep drilling results will be incorporated into an updated mineral resource and mineral reserve statement to be released this year.

• CEO said: “The latest results from our deep drilling programme reinforce the geological strength of Blanket mine and demonstrate the continuity of mineralisation at depth across multiple orebodies. The consistency of grades and widths we are seeing...provides growing confidence in the scale and quality of the mineral resource below the current lowest levels of the mine.”

• CEO said: “These outcomes support our longer term planning efforts and highlight the value of the investments we have made”.

• Announced commencement of a third share buyback programme (the “2026 Programme”) following its FY results release on 17 March 2026.

• Maximum aggregate market value to be bought back: £10,000,000 (excluding associated costs and stamp duty).

• Programme start date: 07 April 2026.

• Programme end date: on or before 31 December 2026.

• Stated sole purpose: reduce share capital.

• Company intends to cancel any shares purchased.

• Entered into non-discretionary instructions with Investec Bank plc to conduct the programme.

• Investec will act as principal and make trading decisions independently of Wickes.

• Programme is within existing AGM authority, which at the date of announcement equals 16,502,960 ordinary shares.

• Said the maximum aggregate number of shares purchasable will be updated to reflect authority granted at the 2026 AGM (19 May 2026).

• Programme to be conducted in accordance with Market Abuse Regulation (596/2014) (as retained in UK law) and the FCA Listing Rules.

time_period: Management appointments effective 02 April 2026; strategy work referenced "beyond" 2027 targets
body:
• Appointed Carol Howle as Deputy Chief Executive Officer, effective 02 April 2026.
  • Howle will continue to lead Supply, Trading & Shipping.
  • In the new role, Howle will oversee BP's ongoing portfolio review and strategic development.
  • BP notes Howle has spent 25 years with the company.
  • BP says Howle served as interim CEO in December 2025.
  • Meg O'Neill (described as CEO since 01 April 2026) said: "As I step into my new role I am committed to providing clear direction and consistency so our company can move forward with confidence."
  • Meg O'Neill said: "To support this, Carol will oversee the ongoing portfolio review and long-term strategy development beyond our 2027 targets. I'm looking forward to working closely with her to deliver a simpler, stronger, more valuable BP to maximize value for our shareholders."

time_period: Motor finance redress scheme update (as at 2 April 2026)
body:
• Provision for the motor finance issue remains GBP1.95bn; Lloyds said it has "undertaken an assessment of the implications and impact of the final rules" and that "the group does not currently believe any change to the provision for this issue is required."
  • FCA confirmed the industry-wide motor finance redress scheme on 30 March 2026.
  • FCA eligibility estimate tightened to 12.1m agreements (from 14.2m at consultation; -14.8%).
  • FCA expected total compensation ("redress") GBP7.5bn (from GBP8.2bn at consultation; -8.5%) and total industry cost (incl. admin) GBP9.1bn (from GBP11.0bn; -17.3%).
  • FCA says the scheme is being streamlined (incl. not requiring recorded delivery), claiming it cuts delivery cost to firms by >40%; also notes "in around 1 in 3 cases compensation will be capped."
  • Lloyds flagged residual uncertainty (response rates, litigation, operational costs): "The ultimate outcome may also differ dependent upon potential actions by various parties, including legal proceedings and complaints. An update will be provided as appropriate with the first quarter results at the end of April."
  • Lloyds Q1 results date: 29 April 2026.
  • Share move on the day: -1.6% to 96.18p, but up >5% over the week.
  • Shore Capital view: stance "appears reasonable" given FCA tweaks, but the key risks remain litigation and take-up; Shore also highlights the FCA's assumed participation rate moving to 75% from 85% (-11.8%) and estimates expected remediation costs for banks down to ~GBP5.2bn from ~GBP5.6bn.
  • External/legal overhang cited in press: Black Horse exposure includes a reported lawsuit involving ~30,000 customers seeking GBP66m.

time_period: March 2026 traffic statistics (and rolling 12 months to end-March 2026)
body:
• March passengers 15.8m (+5.3% YoY); load factor 93% (flat YoY).
  • March flights operated: 88,000+.
  • Rolling 12-month passengers 208.4m (+4.1% YoY); load factor 94% (flat YoY).
  • O'Leary on timing/risk of supply disruption: "But if the war continues, we do run the risk of supply disruptions in Europe in May and June and obviously we hope the war will finish sooner than that and that the risk to supply will be eliminated."
  • O'Leary on scenario outcomes: "It's almost impossible to know. If the war finishes and the Strait of Hormuz is opened by the middle or end of April, then there's no risk to supply."
  • O'Leary on potential scale of impact: "If the war continues, and the disruption to supply continues, we think there is a reasonable risk that maybe 10%, 20%, 25% of our supplies might be at risk through May and June."
  • O'Leary concluding: "So, like everybody else in the industry, we hope this war ends sooner rather than later."
  • Shares (early Thursday): -1.6% to EUR24.56.
  • Additional context from Reuters: Ryanair has warned prolonged conflict could force summer capacity reductions/cancellations if fuel availability tightens; it has also pointed to hedging and monitoring suppliers closely.

body:

Barbara has brought great insight, rigour and sound judgement to her role, and her service on the Audit and Risk, Remuneration and Nomination Committees has been highly valued. We are grateful for her significant commitment and dedication, and we wish her every success in the future."

Time Period Transaction completion (effective 01 April 2026)

• Completed its long-term silver streaming transaction with Wheaton Precious Metals covering BHP’s share of silver production from the Antamina Mine (Peru). ()

• BHP received US$4.3bn total upfront consideration.

• In exchange, BHP will transfer to Wheaton the equivalent of 33.75% of Antamina silver production (subject to a fixed payable rate of 90%), plus ongoing production transfer payments set at 20% of the spot silver price per ounce delivered under the agreement. ()

• After 100 million ounces have been delivered to Wheaton, the stream reduces to 22.5% of Antamina silver production for the remaining life of mine.

• Settlement is via metal credits (i.e. no physical delivery of silver to Wheaton).

Time Period High Court trial in February 2026; judgment issued 01 April 2026 ()

• easyGroup brought a trademark claim against Easyfeetstore (Estonia) and Easyfeet Inc (US) (orthopaedic/orthotic insoles), plus shareholder/director Andriy Klishyn. ()

• Judge Richard Hacon dismissed the claim; found “easy” was the only similarity and there was “no likelihood” of confusion. ()

• Judge: “I have found that there is no relevant similarity…” and (for six of seven trademarks) “no similarities in goods or services.” ()

• Judge also found no “detriment” to easyGroup and no “unfair advantage”. ()

• Context noted: easyGroup has 380+ “easy” branded ventures/websites (easyJet, easyBus, easyHotel etc.) and has pursued multiple “easy” trademark disputes. ()

• Company response / appeal intent (spokesperson quote): “We strongly believe that this judgment is wrong because it undermines the ability of owners of famous families of brands, such as the easy family of brands, to protect consumers from confusion.

“The other side wants consumers to believe that they have something to do with the official easy family of brands when they do not.

“We will appeal and will overturn it, as we have done many times in the past.”

Time Period Transaction completion (effective date 1 April 2026)

• BHP confirmed it has completed the long-term silver streaming agreement with Wheaton (following the earlier announcement on 17 Feb 2026). ()

• BHP has received the US$4.3bn upfront consideration from Wheaton. ()

• In return, BHP will transfer exposure equivalent to 33.75% of Antamina silver production (subject to 90% payable rate) until 100Moz delivered; thereafter stream reduces to 22.5%. ()

• Ongoing production transfer payments are 20% of spot silver price per ounce delivered. ()

• Mechanics: “The settlement of the stream is completed via metal credits with no physical delivery of silver to Wheaton.” ()
On 2 April, CFO & Chief Sustainability Officer Nicholas Cadbury sold 225,000 shares at 362.3p, total GBP815,175.

• Deposit count (Equiniti Trust Company, LLC): 17,577,250 ordinary shares were properly tendered and not properly withdrawn at or below $92.00 per share.

• Notes that not all shares tendered via notice of guaranteed delivery were delivered within the required settlement period.

• Accepted for payment 17,577,250 shares (all properly tendered and not properly withdrawn) at $92.00 per share.

• Aggregate repurchase cost stated as ~$1.617 billion, excluding fees and expenses.

• Repurchased shares represent ~29.7% of shares outstanding as of April 1, 2026.
